# t22.3 - Re-run conformance review after final parity closure

## Summary

Re-ran the final conformance gate against the latest parity evidence bundle after `t21.2.2`.
The seeded DB2 parity rerun closes the previously open primitive JAX-RS echo, operator,
and scenario-action regressions that were still blocking `t21.1`.

The gate still remains FAIL because the required upstream PM closure artifact `t21.3` has not
been published, so there is no authoritative product-signoff verdict for the post-`t21.2.2`
evidence bundle. Independent review of the current artifacts also still leaves the remaining
directly reachable documentation, alternate-page, and primitive-launcher/image inventory only
partially evidenced.

## Deliverables

- [migration-summary.md](./migration-summary.md) - refreshed binary conformance verdict for the post-`t21.2.2` state
- [checkpoints/tasks-to-impl.yaml](./checkpoints/tasks-to-impl.yaml) - aggregate completeness checkpoint updated to include `t21.2.2`
- [checkpoints/traceability-matrix.yaml](./checkpoints/traceability-matrix.yaml) - traceability package refreshed for the latest evidence delta

## Verdict

- Gate: FAIL
- Critical findings: 2
- High findings: 1

## Findings

1. CRITICAL - Missing upstream parity-closure artifact. `t22.3` depends on `t21.3`, but no `t21.3-pm.md` artifact exists for the post-`t21.2.2` evidence bundle.
2. CRITICAL - The current evidence still does not provide PM-approved per-surface closure for the remaining directly reachable documentation, alternate-page, primitive-launcher, and image-page inventory that `t3` keeps in scope.
3. HIGH - Final traceability remains partial for `20` of `30` requirements because the current sign-off package still lacks authoritative closure for the remaining directly reachable surface inventory and its associated observable behaviors.

## Why The Verdict Did Not Flip

- `t21.2.2` materially improves the conformance picture: it proves the preserved primitive JAX-RS echo endpoints, the canonical operator actions, and the full seeded scenario-driver action set on the DB2 primary lane.
- Those evidence gains are enough to retire the old admin/scenario and primitive-echo blockers carried forward from `t21.1`.
- They are not enough to bypass the missing upstream PM rerun artifact or to infer final parity closure for the broader directly reachable documentation and alternate-surface inventory.

## Validation Basis

- Reviewed `t21.1-pm.md` as the latest available PM parity verdict.
- Reviewed `t21.2.2-tester.md` as the latest seeded DB2 parity evidence delta.
- Verified that no `t21.3*` artifact exists under `artifacts/`.
- Re-synchronized the completeness summary and aggregate checkpoints to the current blocker state.

## Test Results

- Command: artifact review only; no executable validation command was available for the missing upstream PM artifact dependency
- Passed: 0
- Failed: 0
- Skipped: 1
